title: PM Lane 322 - Project Miner Temp Power Customer Delivery And Durable Proof Execution Gate Design No-Live Packet
authority_basis: Standing PM blocker authority and repo technical authority, used here to convert Lane 321's request design into a dispatch-only execution-gate contract without admitting implementation or request send.
activation_condition: Lane 321 already defines the exact future mutation route, envelope, payload, and paired readback proof.
No Temp Power customer delivery and durable proof execution-gate packet exists yet.
The next blocker after lane 321 is admission shape, not current implementation failure.
current_truth: Lane 315 remains the hosted-green runtime baseline and still blocks delivery execution in the current customer-preview review slice.
Lane 321 now defines the exact future request contract for one future delivery/proof review route, but that route is not yet admitted for implementation or execution.

The next bounded design step is an execution gate only: define the exact admission phrase, the forced stop conditions when that phrase is absent, the narrow sequence a later executor may follow if the phrase is explicitly present, and the unchanged downstream proof that must remain true after any future execution.
Finance/payroll/billing/invoice/accounting outputs, source writeback, and customer billing delivery remain outside this gate.
This gate governs at most one separately packeted first-write sequence for one future delivery/proof review route.
allowed_actions: Define the exact required admission phrase for future delivery/proof first-write work.
Define the allowed future executor sequence, forced stop conditions, covered route, and required post-execution proof.
Keep the gate no-live and design-only.
not_allowed: No product code, schema, route implementation, or hosted action.
No request send, persistence execution, or runtime route admission.
No finance, payroll, billing, invoice, accounting, external finance sync, source writeback, or customer billing delivery work.
No auth, ingress, DNS, or secret changes.
validation: Decision label, selected outcome, exact admission phrase, forced stop conditions, covered route, post-execution proof, and blocked-domain boundaries are present across touched lane 322 files
Canonical PM docs/status route the next follow-on step through a local mocked request dry run rather than direct implementation admission
git diff --check passes for touched lane 322 and canonical PM doc surfaces
validation_result: PASS: lane 322 defines the future delivery/proof execution gate and keeps implementation, runtime delivery, finance, source-writeback, and customer-billing widening deferred until an exact separate admission phrase is present.
